chosen_action.title: Reduce marketing-active-loop cadence from every 2 hours to every 4 hours

shared_findings_used: MARKETING_SELF_IMPROVEMENT.md: outcomes matter more than activity
MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: active tactics that stay flat should be treated as failing
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: current bottleneck is interest-to-free-use conversion, not more activity
market_intelligence_latest.json: reuse shared findings instead of siloed resets
marketing_workflow_audit_latest.md: recent actions already saturated same-family outreach windows
adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg stars/watchers/forks flat in the recent window
distribution_lane_latest.json: current lane is measurement_hold; another reset would be fake progress
outreach-log.md: multiple fresh submissions/outreach items already live and awaiting review
why_this_action: The loop was still scheduled often enough to reward measurement-hold churn instead of distinct outcome-bearing work. With external windows already saturated and Codeberg adoption flat, reducing active-loop cadence is a higher-leverage repair than another same-window hold pass.
expected_outcome: Fewer fake-progress runs, cleaner measurement windows, and more distinct high-leverage marketing actions when the loop does wake.
measurement_window: Assess by 2026-05-31 whether measurement-hold/follow-through logs decrease and whether the next 7 days produce clearer outcome-bearing actions or Codeberg adoption movement.
replacement_condition: If reducing cadence causes missed live opportunities or slower external execution without clearer outcome movement, replace it with lane-specific schedules instead of a single generic active loop cadence.
